title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: ggplot2 successful data visualization library ever built. designed 2005–2007 world static print graphics. web, large data, interactive dashboards changed “plotting” means. glyph doesn’t try replace ggplot2 — asks: grammar graphics look like designed 2026?

title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: Every glyph visualization specification — pure R list fully describes plot without executing rendering. single important architectural decision root cause advantages: specs matter: - Serializable: jsonlite::toJSON(compile(spec)$spec) gives JSON blob can send server, save file, embed document. - Inspectable: can print, diff, test specs without rendering. - Multi-backend: One spec compiles D3, Canvas, WebGL, SVG, PDF. - Interoperable: spec maps closely Vega-Lite, enabling R↔︎JS↔︎Python workflows. ggplot2’s ggplot objects contain grobs (grid graphics objects) tightly coupled R’s graphics device system. can’t serialize ggplot JSON, render browser without conversion, inspect cleanly.
code: glyph_spec = {   data:      data.frame or reference   mappings:  list of quosures (lazy, not evaluated)   marks:     list of mark descriptors   scales:    list of scale configs   interact:  list of interaction configs   animate:   animation config   theme:     token map   layout:    composition config }

title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: ggplot2 requires aes() create mapping object. historical artifact NSE R less mature. rlang’s quosure system, can capture bare names directly: Mappings stored quosures (expressions + environments) evaluated compile(). means spec portable doesn’t carry data references prematurely.
code: # ggplot2 ggplot(data, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl)))  # glyph glyph(data, x = wt, y = mpg)  # global mappings   |> mark_point(color = cyl)    # per-mark mappings

title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: ggplot2 separates geoms (visual forms) stats (data transforms). geom_smooth() really stat_smooth() + geom_line(). elegant theory confusing practice (“use geom_bar() stat_count()?”). glyph uses simpler model: marks visual encodings. Statistical transforms explicit, composable steps applied data marking: makes data flow explicit: raw data → transform → mark. hidden stat layers.
code: glyph(data, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   transform_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE) |>  # planned   mark_ribbon(y_min = .lower, y_max = .upper) |>   mark_line(y = .fitted)

title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: key insight Vega-Lite: interactions selections. tooltip single-point selection display action. Brushing multi-point selection. Zooming continuous interval selection position channels. glyph encodes interactions part spec, runtime behaviors bolted rendering: means: - Interactions survive export (HTML output self-contained) - spec can describe interactions without knowing backend - Server-side tools (Shiny) can intercept selection events
code: interact(   tooltip = TRUE,     # point selection → show values   brush = TRUE,       # interval selection → highlight   zoom = TRUE,        # interval selection → rescale   crossfilter = TRUE  # propagate selection to linked views )

title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: ggplot2’s theme() 90+ arguments treats every visual property independently. glyph uses design tokens — small set semantic values cascade visualization: Setting bg = "#1a1a2e" (dark) automatically gives : - Light text (fg = "#e0e0e0") - Subtle grid lines (grid_color = "#2a2a4a") - Appropriate tooltip styling CSS custom properties work, ’s far maintainable flat theme arguments.
code: font       →  all text elements font_size  →  base size; title = font_size × title_size bg         →  plot background; auto-derives fg for contrast fg         →  text, axis lines, ticks accent     →  single-series mark color grid       →  grid visibility and color (derived from bg) padding    →  all margins uniformly

title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: ggplot2 delegates multi-plot layouts patchwork, cowplot, gridExtra. glyph includes compose() layout part visualization design, afterthought: layout part spec. means: - Linked selections can propagate across panels - Shared scales computed globally, per-panel - entire composition exports one self-contained HTML
code: compose(p1, p2, p3,         type = "wrap",         linked_selections = TRUE,         shared_scales = "x")

title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: compile() step translates abstract spec backend-specific render tree: compiler auto-selects based data size can overridden. backend implements mark/scale/interaction semantics; rendering primitives differ.
code: ┌──► D3.js + SVG (< 10K points)                     │ glyph_spec ──►  compile() ──► Canvas 2D (10K–100K points)                     │                     ├──► WebGL / regl (100K+ points)                     │                     └──► Static SVG / PDF (export)

title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: ggplot2’s stat system deeply integrated covers dozens methods. Replicating multi-year effort. glyph’s strategy: start explicit transform_*() functions common cases (smooth, bin, density, aggregate) grow .

title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: ggplot2 200+ extension packages. glyph can’t compete breadth. Instead, Vega-Lite export path provides escape hatch: anything glyph can’t , Vega-Lite/D3 probably can. spec-first design also makes extension easier — add new mark type without touching renderer.

title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: R’s PDF/SVG graphics devices tuned decades. glyph’s static export (via headless Chrome rendering HTML output) never match R’s native device quality print. publication-quality static plots, ggplot2 remains right tool.

title: glyph — Architecture & Design Decisions
text: cold truth: R visualization solved problem users. Convincing people learn new grammar requires either dramatically better experience (possible) institutional adoption (hard). glyph’s best path complement — used ggplot2 falls short — replacement.

title: glyph vs ggplot2: Side-by-Side Comparison
text: ggplot2: glyph: changed: aes(), factor() coercion, + operator. Pipeline reads left--right |>. Color scale one function call.
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(     title = "Motor Trend Cars",     x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",     y = "Miles per Gallon",     color = "Cylinders"   ) +   theme_minimal() glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl, style = list(size = 6)) |>   scale_color("Set2") |>   scale("x", label = "Weight (1000 lbs)") |>   scale("y", label = "Miles per Gallon") |>   titles(title = "Motor Trend Cars") |>   theme_tokens(preset = "minimal")

title: glyph vs ggplot2: Side-by-Side Comparison
text: ggplot2 + plotly: glyph: changed: lossy ggplotly() conversion. Interactions part spec, post-hoc wrapper. Tooltip template simple string, paste() call inside aes().
code:
library(plotly) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl), text = paste("Car:", rownames(mtcars), "
MPG:", mpg, "
Weight:", wt))) + geom_point(size = 3) ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text") # Note: loses theme, some formatting; no brush-to-filter glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |> mark_point(color = cyl) |> interact( tooltip = "Car: {.rownames}\nMPG: {mpg}\nWeight: {wt}", zoom = TRUE, brush = TRUE, hover = "enlarge" ) # Theme, formatting, all interactions preserved — no conversion step

title: glyph vs ggplot2: Side-by-Side Comparison
text: ggplot2 + gganimate: glyph: changed: Animation one function call pipeline, separate package. Output interactive HTML (play/pause/scrub), static GIF. Tooltips work animation.
code: library(gganimate)  p <- ggplot(gapminder, aes(x = continent, y = lifeExp, fill = continent)) +   geom_col(stat = "summary", fun = "mean") +   transition_states(year, transition_length = 2, state_length = 1) +   labs(title = "Year: {closest_state}") +   theme_minimal() +   enter_grow() +   ease_aes("bounce-out")  animate(p, fps = 20, width = 600, height = 400) # Renders to GIF (not interactive) glyph(gapminder, x = continent, y = lifeExp) |>   mark_bar() |>   animate(by = year, transition = "morph", easing = "bounce") |>   interact(tooltip = TRUE) |>   titles(title = "Life Expectancy by Continent") |>   theme_tokens(preset = "minimal") # Renders as interactive HTML with playback controls

title: glyph vs ggplot2: Side-by-Side Comparison
text: ggplot2 + patchwork: glyph: changed: extra package. Linked selections across panels via linked_selections = TRUE. Brush one panel cross-filters others.
code: library(patchwork)  p1 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() + theme_minimal() p2 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(hp, mpg)) + geom_point() + theme_minimal() p3 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) + geom_boxplot() + theme_minimal()  (p1 | p2) / p3 +   plot_annotation(title = "Motor Trend Dashboard") # No linked interactions between panels p1 <- glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl) |>   interact(brush = TRUE)  p2 <- glyph(mtcars, x = hp, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl) |>   interact(brush = TRUE)  p3 <- glyph(mtcars, x = cyl, y = mpg) |>   mark_bar()  compose(p1, p2, p3,         type = "wrap",         linked_selections = TRUE,         title = "Motor Trend Dashboard") # Brushing in p1 highlights the same cars in p2 and p3

title: glyph vs ggplot2: Side-by-Side Comparison
text: ggplot2 + ggExtra: glyph: changed: Built-. One function call. Size configurable.
code: library(ggExtra)  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point() +   theme_minimal()  ggMarginal(p, type = "histogram", groupColour = TRUE) glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl) |>   marginals(x = "histogram", y = "density", size = 0.2)

title: glyph vs ggplot2: Side-by-Side Comparison
text: ggplot2: glyph: custom colors:
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(color = "#6ec6ff") +   theme(     plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#1a1a2e"),     panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#1a1a2e"),     panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#2a2a4a"),     panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),     axis.text = element_text(color = "#e0e0e0"),     axis.title = element_text(color = "#e0e0e0"),     text = element_text(color = "#e0e0e0")   ) # 9 lines of theme overrides glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point() |>   theme_tokens(preset = "dark") # 1 line. All contrast/grid/text colors auto-derived. glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point() |>   theme_tokens(bg = "#1a1a2e")   # fg, grid_color, accent all adapt automatically

title: glyph vs ggplot2: Side-by-Side Comparison
text: ggplot2: glyph:
code: ggsave("plot.png", width = 8, height = 6)   # static only ggsave("plot.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)   # static only ggsave("plot.svg", width = 8, height = 6)   # static only # No interactive HTML export. No spec export. spec <- glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point() |>   interact(tooltip = TRUE, zoom = TRUE)  export(spec, "plot.html")              # interactive HTML export(spec, "plot.svg")               # static SVG export(spec, "plot.json")             # raw spec (inspect/debug) cat(to_vegalite(spec))                 # Vega-Lite JSON (use in Python/JS)

title: glyph vs ggplot2: Side-by-Side Comparison
text: Use ggplot2 : - need specific extension (ggridges, ggalluvial, etc.) - ’re producing static plots print/PDF - want maximum community support StackOverflow answers - Statistical transforms (smooth, density2d) central workflow Use glyph : - Interactivity part deliverable (dashboards, reports, exploration) - want linked views without Shiny - Animation important (presentations, storytelling) - ’re working large datasets (>10K points) - want cleaner, composable API - need export specs JavaScript (Vega-Lite, D3) - Theme consistency across many plots matters (token system)

title: Getting Started with glyph
text: aes(), factor() coercion — aesthetic mappings just bare column names passed straight glyph() mark_point().
code: glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl) |>   render()

what: 2. Tooltips and hover, declared in the pipeline
title: Getting Started with glyph
text: Interactivity grammar, glue. interact() turns tooltips hover effect right plot built, titles() adds title pipe — ggplotly() conversion step, lost formatting. Hover point see enlarge; hover longer see tooltip.
code: glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl) |>   interact(tooltip = TRUE, hover = "enlarge") |>   titles(title = "Motor Trend Cars") |>   render()

title: Getting Started with glyph
text: animate() declares transition part spec. stagger offsets bar’s entrance animation draw sequence rather . Reload page (re-run chunk R session) see bars slide .
code: glyph(mtcars, x = cyl, y = mpg) |>   mark_bar() |>   animate(transition = "slide", stagger = 50) |>   render()

title: Getting Started with glyph
text: Instead ggplot2’s dozens individual theme() arguments, theme_tokens() takes small preset (individual tokens like bg, font, accent) cascades foreground, grid, title colors automatically contrast.
code: glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl) |>   interact(tooltip = TRUE) |>   theme_tokens(preset = "dark") |>   titles(title = "Dark Theme Example") |>   render()

title: Getting Started with glyph
text: compose() arranges multiple glyph_spec objects single layout — , two scatterplots side side — without reaching patchwork cowplot. Composed layouts rendered way single spec: pass layout render().
code: p1 <- glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |> mark_point(color = cyl) p2 <- glyph(mtcars, x = hp, y = mpg) |> mark_point(color = cyl)  compose(p1, p2, type = "hstack") |>   render()

title: Getting Started with glyph
text: Browse function reference every mark, scale, layout primitive glyph provides. Read glyph vs ggplot2: Side--Side Comparison broader tour two grammars differ.

code: @Manual{,   title = {glyph: A Next-Generation Grammar of Interactive Graphics},   author = {Joash Joshua Ayo},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0, https://josh45-source.github.io/glyph/},   url = {https://github.com/josh45-source/glyph}, }

what: The grammar of graphics, rebuilt for a web that moves.
title: glyph: A Next-Generation Grammar of Interactive Graphics
text: glyph next-generation grammar interactive graphics R. treats interactivity, animation, composable layouts first-class concepts — afterthoughts bolted via chain extension packages.
code: install.packages("glyph")

title: glyph: A Next-Generation Grammar of Interactive Graphics
text: plot. One needs plotly, ggrepel, paragraph theme() calls get interactive, styled, readable. needs pipe. aes(). factor() coercion. + juggling geometry, scales, theme one flat expression. get zoom, brush, hover free.
code: library(ggplot2) library(plotly)  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(       x = wt, y = mpg,       color = factor(cyl),       text = paste0(         cyl, " cyl, ", mpg,         " mpg at ", wt, " tons")     )) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(title = "Motor Trend Cars",        subtitle = "Weight vs fuel efficiency") +   theme(     text = element_text(       family = "Inter", size = 12),     plot.background = element_rect(       fill = "#1a1a2e"),     panel.background = element_rect(       fill = "#1a1a2e"),     axis.text = element_text(       color = "#e0e0e0"),     panel.grid = element_line(       color = "#2a2a4a")   )  ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text") # loses some theming; no zoom/brush library(glyph)  glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl) |>   scale_color("Set2") |>   interact(     tooltip = "{cyl} cyl, {mpg} mpg at {wt} tons",     zoom = TRUE,     hover = "enlarge"   ) |>   theme_tokens(bg = "#1a1a2e", font = "Inter") |>   titles(     title = "Motor Trend Cars",     subtitle = "Weight vs fuel efficiency"   )     # fg, grid, and title color are # auto-derived for contrast   # tooltip + zoom + brush, built in

title: glyph: A Next-Generation Grammar of Interactive Graphics
text: Interactivity grammar, glue. Tooltips, zoom, brushing, linked views declared right pipeline interact() selection() — stapled afterward plotly::ggplotly(). spec plot. Every glyph visualization pure, inspectable data structure. Serialize JSON, export Vega-Lite to_vegalite(), compile() HTML, SVG, canvas, WebGL — without touching code built . Composition built . Multi-plot layouts, marginal distributions, inset plots, linked brushing across panels ship compose(), marginals(), inset() — patchwork, ggExtra. Tokens, 90 theme arguments. theme_tokens() takes handful design tokens — font, background, grid — cascades foreground, grid, title colors automatically contrast. Animation pipeline step. animate() declares transitions, stagger, easing right plot built, separate gganimate render pass.

title: glyph: A Next-Generation Grammar of Interactive Graphics
code: install.packages("glyph")  # Development version # remotes::install_github("josh45-source/glyph")

title: glyph: A Next-Generation Grammar of Interactive Graphics
text: Read Getting Started guide live, interactive examples, side--side comparison ggplot2, browse function reference see everything glyph can .
code: library(glyph)  glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl, size = hp) |>   interact(tooltip = "{cyl} cyl, {mpg} mpg at {wt} tons", zoom = TRUE) |>   scale("y", zero = TRUE, label = "Miles per gallon") |>   theme_tokens(preset = "dark") |>   titles(title = "Motor Trend Cars", subtitle = "Weight vs fuel efficiency")

title: Add animation/transition behavior — animate
text: Add animation/transition behavior

title: Add animation/transition behavior — animate
code: animate(   spec,   transition = "morph",   duration = 500,   stagger = 0,   by = NULL,   easing = "ease-in-out",   loop = FALSE )

title: Add animation/transition behavior — animate
text: spec glyph_spec transition Transition type: "morph", "fade", "slide", "none" duration Duration milliseconds stagger Delay successive marks ms (entrance effects) Column defines animation keyframes (like gganimate's transition_states). e.g. = year plays years. easing Easing function: "linear", "ease-", "ease-", "ease--", "bounce", "elastic" loop Repeat animation (TRUE, FALSE, number times)

title: Add animation/transition behavior — animate
text: Modified glyph_spec

title: Add animation/transition behavior — animate
code: # Entrance animation: bars grow from baseline glyph(mtcars, x = cyl, y = mpg) |>   mark_bar() |>   animate(transition = "slide", stagger = 50) #> <glyph_spec: 32 x 11, 1 mark(s)>  # Keyframe animation: morph between groups glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(size = hp, color = cyl) |>   animate(by = gear, transition = "morph", duration = 800) #> <glyph_spec: 32 x 11, 1 mark(s)>

title: Animation Grammar — animation
text: Animations glyph declarative state transitions, frame--frame rendering. describe changes interpolate, renderer handles rest.

title: Animation Grammar — animation
text: Two animation paradigms: Transitions: animate data states (e.g. year 2020 → 2021). Marks morph smoothly, entering/exiting data changes. Entrances: animate marks appearing first render (stagger bars growing zero, points fading ).

title: Compile a glyph spec to a resolved representation — compile

title: Compile a glyph spec to a resolved representation — compile

title: Compile a glyph spec to a resolved representation — compile
text: spec glyph_spec glyph_layout engine Rendering backend: "auto", "html", "svg", "canvas", "webgl" width Width pixels (NULL auto) height Height pixels (NULL auto)

title: Compile a glyph spec to a resolved representation — compile
text: glyph_compiled object (list resolved data + JSON) glyph_compiled object containing resolved spec, JSON string, engine selection glyph_compiled object containing resolved layout spec JSON string

title: Compose multiple glyph specs into a layout — compose
text: Compose multiple glyph specs layout

title: Compose multiple glyph specs into a layout — compose
code: compose(   ...,   type = "hstack",   widths = NULL,   heights = NULL,   gap = 10,   shared_scales = FALSE,   linked_selections = FALSE,   title = NULL )

title: Compose multiple glyph specs into a layout — compose
text: ... glyph_spec objects layout objects (nesting) type Layout type: "hstack", "vstack", "grid", "wrap" widths Relative widths columns (e.g. c(2, 1) 2:1 split) heights Relative heights rows gap Gap plots px shared_scales Share axis scales across plots: TRUE, FALSE, "x", "y", "" linked_selections Share interaction selections across plots title Overall layout title

title: Compose multiple glyph specs into a layout — compose
text: glyph_layout object

title: Compose multiple glyph specs into a layout — compose
code: p1 <- glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |> mark_point() p2 <- glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = hp) |> mark_point() p3 <- glyph(mtcars, x = hp, y = mpg) |> mark_line()  # Horizontal stack compose(p1, p2, type = "hstack") #>  #> ── Glyph Layout ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: hstack #> • Panels: 2 #> • Linked selections: FALSE  # Grid with linked brushing compose(p1, p2, p3, type = "wrap",         linked_selections = TRUE, shared_scales = "x") #>  #> ── Glyph Layout ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: wrap #> • Panels: 3 #> • Linked selections: TRUE

title: Facet a plot by one or two variables — facet
text: Modified glyph_spec

what: glyph: A Next-Generation Grammar of Interactive Graphics — glyph-package
title: glyph: A Next-Generation Grammar of Interactive Graphics — glyph-package
text: modern visualization grammar treats interactivity, animation, composable layouts first-class concepts rather afterthoughts. Designed address key limitations existing grammars: native hover, click, zoom events, 'WebGL'-accelerated rendering large datasets, built-multi-plot composition, token-based theming system. Renders interactive HTML widgets via 'D3.js' static SVG single declarative specification.

title: glyph: A Next-Generation Grammar of Interactive Graphics — glyph-package
text: Maintainer: Joash Joshua Ayo joashjoshua789@gmail.com

title: Create a Glyph Visualization — glyph
text: Entry point glyph grammar. Creates specification object describes visualization declaratively. Unlike ggplot2, spec pure data structure (nested list) can serialized, inspected, compiled multiple backends.

title: Create a Glyph Visualization — glyph
code: glyph(data = NULL, ...)

title: Create a Glyph Visualization — glyph
text: data data.frame tibble. Unlike ggplot2, glyph also accepts lists--lists, URLs CSV/JSON, arrow tables (planned). ... Global aesthetic mappings using bare column names (aes() needed). e.g. glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg)

title: Create a Glyph Visualization — glyph
text: glyph_spec object

title: Create a Glyph Visualization — glyph
code: spec <- glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point() summary(spec) #>  #> ── Glyph Specification ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Data: 32 x 11 #> • Marks: 1 #> • Interactions: 0 #> • Animated: FALSE

what: Add interactive behaviors to a glyph spec — interact
title: Add interactive behaviors to a glyph spec — interact
text: Add interactive behaviors glyph spec

title: Add interactive behaviors to a glyph spec — interact
code: interact(   spec,   tooltip = FALSE,   zoom = FALSE,   brush = FALSE,   hover = NULL,   click = NULL,   crossfilter = FALSE,   nearest = FALSE )

title: Add interactive behaviors to a glyph spec — interact
text: spec glyph_spec tooltip Show values hover. TRUE auto-generated, glue-style template string like "{x}: {y} ({color})". zoom Enable scroll--zoom pan brush Enable rectangular brush selection hover Highlight mark hover ("enlarge", "brighten", "outline", NULL) click Action click: "select", "filter", "url", callback name crossfilter Link plot's selections plots layout nearest Snap selection nearest point (useful line charts)

title: Add interactive behaviors to a glyph spec — interact
text: Modified glyph_spec

title: Add interactive behaviors to a glyph spec — interact
code: glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl) |>   interact(     tooltip = "{cyl} cylinders\n{mpg} mpg at {wt} tons",     zoom = TRUE,     hover = "enlarge",     brush = TRUE,     crossfilter = TRUE   ) #> <glyph_spec: 32 x 11, 1 mark(s)>

title: First-Class Interactivity — interactivity
text: Unlike ggplot2 interactivity bolted via ggplotly(), glyph treats interaction part visualization grammar. Interactions declared spec compiled appropriate backend (D3 events HTML, Shiny bindings server-side).

title: First-Class Interactivity — interactivity
text: Design philosophy: interactions selections filter highlight marks. follows Vega-Lite's insight interactions really defining subsets data. tooltip "select nearest point, show values." Brushing "select points rectangle, highlight ."

title: Layout Composition — layout
text: Built-multi-plot composition. external packages needed. Layouts can express grids, stacks, insets, marginal plots, arbitrary nesting. Composed plots can share selections cross-filtering (linked brushing).

what: Add a marginal plot (histogram/density on axes) — marginals
title: Add a marginal plot (histogram/density on axes) — marginals
text: Adds marginal distributions plot's axes — common pattern requires ggExtra manual grid manipulation ggplot2.

title: Add a marginal plot (histogram/density on axes) — marginals
code: marginals(spec, x = "histogram", y = "histogram", size = 0.15)

title: Add a marginal plot (histogram/density on axes) — marginals
text: spec glyph_spec x Marginal type x-axis: "histogram", "density", "boxplot", NULL y Marginal type y-axis: "histogram", "density", "boxplot", NULL size Proportion plot area marginals (0.0 0.4)

title: Add a marginal plot (histogram/density on axes) — marginals
text: Modified glyph_spec

title: Add a marginal plot (histogram/density on axes) — marginals
code: glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl) |>   marginals(x = "histogram", y = "density") #> <glyph_spec: 32 x 11, 1 mark(s)>

title: Visual Marks — marks
text: Marks visual encodings glyph — analogous ggplot2's geoms key differences: Mappings without aes(): pass bare column names directly. Per-mark data: mark can data source, enabling multi-dataset plots without awkward data parameter overrides. Built-interaction hints: marks carry interaction metadata (happens hover, click, drag) part spec. Transition-aware: marks know interpolate states animation.

title: Rendering Pipeline — rendering
text: rendering pipeline compiles glyph_spec output. spec first resolved (evaluate quosures, compute stats, merge defaults), serialized JSON, handed backend: "html" (default): htmlwidgets + D3.js interactive viewing "svg": Static SVG file (publication quality) "canvas": HTML5 Canvas large-data performance "webgl": WebGL via regl/deck.gl 100K+ points (planned) "pdf": Direct PDF output via R's pdf() device (planned)

title: Rendering Pipeline — rendering
text: Key architectural difference ggplot2: spec pure data structure. compile() step resolves concrete render tree. means can: Inspect compiled spec JSON (debugging export) Serialize render different machine Export Vega-Lite JSON (near 1:1 mapping) Compile multiple backends one spec

what: Define a scale for an aesthetic channel — scale
title: Define a scale for an aesthetic channel — scale
text: Define scale aesthetic channel

title: Define a scale for an aesthetic channel — scale
code: scale(   spec,   aesthetic,   type = "auto",   domain = NULL,   range = NULL,   nice = TRUE,   zero = FALSE,   reverse = FALSE,   label = NULL,   format = NULL )

title: Define a scale for an aesthetic channel — scale
text: spec glyph_spec aesthetic channel: "x", "y", "color", "size", "shape", "alpha" type Scale type: "linear", "log", "sqrt", "time", "ordinal", "band", "quantize", "threshold" domain Explicit domain (data range). NULL auto. range Explicit output range. color: palette name vector. nice Round domain nice values (TRUE/FALSE) zero Force zero domain (TRUE/FALSE) reverse Reverse scale label Axis/legend label (NULL auto column name) format Format string tick labels (e.g. "$.2f", "%b %Y")

title: Define a scale for an aesthetic channel — scale
text: Modified glyph_spec

title: Define a scale for an aesthetic channel — scale
code: glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl) |>   scale("y", "linear", zero = TRUE, label = "Miles per gallon") |>   scale("color", "ordinal", range = "Set2") #> <glyph_spec: 32 x 11, 1 mark(s)>

title: Scales — scales
text: Scales map data values visual properties. Glyph simplifies ggplot2's scale_<aesthetic>_<type> explosion composable system: scale(<aesthetic>, <type>, ...).

title: Scales — scales
code: scale_color(spec, palette = "Tableau10", ...)  scale_log(spec, aesthetic = "y", base = 10, ...)  scale_time(spec, aesthetic = "x", ...)

title: Scales — scales
text: spec glyph_spec palette named palette: "viridis", "Set2", "Tableau10", "Blues", etc. ... Additional arguments passed scale() aesthetic channel: "x", "y", "color", "size", "shape", "alpha" base Log base (default 10)

title: Scales — scales
text: Modified glyph_spec object Modified glyph_spec object Modified glyph_spec object

title: Scales — scales
text: scale_color(): Set color palette name scale_log(): Log-transform axis scale_time(): Time/date axis

title: Add a selection parameter (advanced) — selection
text: spec glyph_spec name Selection name (referenced conditional encodings) type "point", "interval", "legend" Event trigger: "click", "mouseover", "drag" fields data fields selection projects onto resolve composed views: "global", "union", "intersect"

title: Add a selection parameter (advanced) — selection
text: Modified glyph_spec

title: Add a selection parameter (advanced) — selection
code: # Interactive legend: click legend entries to filter glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point(color = cyl) |>   selection("legend_filter", type = "legend", fields = "cyl") #> <glyph_spec: 32 x 11, 1 mark(s)>

title: Set theme tokens — theme_tokens
text: Set theme tokens

title: Set theme tokens — theme_tokens
code: theme_tokens(   spec,   preset = NULL,   font = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   bg = NULL,   fg = NULL,   accent = NULL,   grid = NULL,   border = NULL,   padding = NULL,   title_size = NULL )

title: Set theme tokens — theme_tokens
text: spec glyph_spec preset named preset: "light" (default), "dark", "minimal", "publication", "presentation". Presets set tokens coherent defaults. font Font family text font_size Base font size px (axis labels scale relative ) bg Background color fg Primary text/axis color accent Primary accent color (used single-series marks) grid Grid line visibility: TRUE, FALSE, "x", "y" border Plot border: TRUE/FALSE padding Padding around plot area px title_size Title font size multiplier (relative font_size)

title: Set theme tokens — theme_tokens
text: Modified glyph_spec

title: Set theme tokens — theme_tokens
code: glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point() |>   theme_tokens(preset = "dark") #> <glyph_spec: 32 x 11, 1 mark(s)>  glyph(mtcars, x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_point() |>   theme_tokens(font = "IBM Plex Sans", bg = "#fafafa", grid = "y") #> <glyph_spec: 32 x 11, 1 mark(s)>

title: Token-Based Theming — theming
text: Instead ggplot2's 90+ theme() arguments, glyph uses design-token system. small set semantic tokens (font, colors, spacing, sizes) cascade entire visualization. Think like CSS custom properties plots.

title: Token-Based Theming — theming
text: Tokens cascade: setting bg changes background, also automatically adjusts text color contrast, grid line opacity, tooltip styling. override want; everything else adapts.

title: glyph 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-06 Initial CRAN release. Core spec builder quosure-based aesthetic mappings (aes() required). Eight mark types: mark_point(), mark_line(), mark_bar(), mark_area(), mark_text(), mark_rule(), mark_ribbon(), mark_link(). First-class interactivity via interact(): tooltips, zoom, brush selection, hover effects, cross-filtering, named selections. Declarative animation via animate(): entrance transitions keyframe animation data states. Unified scale system via scale() convenience wrappers scale_color(), scale_log(), scale_time(). Token-based theming via theme_tokens() five built-presets: light, dark, minimal, publication, presentation. Built-layout composition via compose(), marginals(), inset(), facet(). Multi-backend compilation pipeline auto engine selection based data size (HTML/D3, Canvas, WebGL). JSON spec export via export() Vega-Lite interoperability via to_vegalite(). D3.js v7 rendering engine via ‘htmlwidgets’.
